title: Delete a node in BST

starterCode.python:

#        self.right = None

class Solution:
    # @param A : root node of tree
    # @param B : integer
    # @return the root node in the tree
    def solve(self, A, B):

hints: Use recursive approach to find and delete the node.
If current node value < B, recursively delete from right subtree.
If current node value > B, recursively delete from left subtree.
If current node value == B, handle three cases:
- One child: return the non-null child
- Two children: replace with in-order predecessor (rightmost node in left subtree), then delete the predecessor.
In-order predecessor is the rightmost node in the left subtree.
